(Welsh pronunciation: [[əˈvɛni]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Welsh "Help:IPA/Welsh")) is a variety of Welsh [cheese](/wiki/Cheese "Cheese"), consisting of [Cheddar cheese](/wiki/Cheddar_cheese "Cheddar cheese") blended with [mustard seed](/wiki/Mustard_seed "Mustard seed") and [ale](/wiki/Ale "Ale"). It takes its name from the [Welsh language](/wiki/Welsh_language "Welsh language") name of [Abergavenny](/wiki/Abergavenny "Abergavenny"), a [market town](/wiki/Market_town "Market town") in [Monmouthshire](/wiki/Monmouthshire "Monmouthshire"), [South East Wales](/wiki/South_East_Wales "South East Wales"). Y Fenni, when coated in red [wax](/wiki/Wax "Wax"), is also known as *Red Dragon*, a name derived from the [dragon](/wiki/Welsh_Dragon "Welsh Dragon") on the [Flag of Wales](/wiki/Flag_of_Wales "Flag of Wales").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Type of Welsh cheese

"Y-Fenni" redirects here. For the settlement known in Welsh as Y Fenni, see [Abergavenny](/wiki/Abergavenny "Abergavenny").

| Y Fenni | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Country of origin | [Wales](/wiki/Wales "Wales") |
| Region | [Monmouthshire](/wiki/Monmouthshire "Monmouthshire") |
| Town | [Abergavenny](/wiki/Abergavenny "Abergavenny") |
| Source of milk | [Cows](/wiki/Cow "Cow") |
| Texture | Firm |
| Named after | [Abergavenny](/wiki/Abergavenny "Abergavenny") |

**Y Fenni** (Welsh pronunciation: [[əˈvɛni]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Welsh "Help:IPA/Welsh")) is a variety of Welsh [cheese](/wiki/Cheese "Cheese"), consisting of [Cheddar cheese](/wiki/Cheddar_cheese "Cheddar cheese") blended with [mustard seed](/wiki/Mustard_seed "Mustard seed") and [ale](/wiki/Ale "Ale"). It takes its name from the [Welsh language](/wiki/Welsh_language "Welsh language") name of [Abergavenny](/wiki/Abergavenny "Abergavenny"), a [market town](/wiki/Market_town "Market town") in [Monmouthshire](/wiki/Monmouthshire "Monmouthshire"), [South East Wales](/wiki/South_East_Wales "South East Wales"). Y Fenni, when coated in red [wax](/wiki/Wax "Wax"), is also known as *Red Dragon*, a name derived from the [dragon](/wiki/Welsh_Dragon "Welsh Dragon") on the [Flag of Wales](/wiki/Flag_of_Wales "Flag of Wales").[[1]](#cite_note-1)
